title: NDFC POAP / ZTP Bootstrap and Day-0 Onboarding Failures
app: Cisco DC Networking Application (Splunkbase 7777), NDFC syslog
spl:
index=cisco_dc sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:poap" earliest=-7d
| where match(lower(status),"(?i)fail|error|timeout") OR (match(lower(stage),"(?i)image|cert|dhcp") AND status!="success")
| stats latest(_time) as last_fail latest(error_code) as err latest(stage) as stage by serial_number, switch_name
| sort -last_fail
| table last_fail, serial_number, switch_name, stage, err
description: Automated provisioning brings switches into the fabric quickly; DHCP, image fetch, or certificate failures during POAP/ZTP delay expansions and leave partially configured devices in racks. Monitoring onboarding outcomes keeps brownfield growth on schedule and prevents rogue devices from sitting outside policy.
value: Automated provisioning brings switches into the fabric quickly; DHCP, image fetch, or certificate failures during POAP/ZTP delay expansions and leave partially configured devices in racks. Monitoring onboarding outcomes keeps brownfield growth on schedule and prevents rogue devices from sitting outside policy.
implementation: (1) Forward NDFC POAP/ZTP logs to Splunk with parsed `stage` milestones. (2) Alert on any failure before switch reaches `In-Sync`. (3) Join serial to inventory (UC-18.4.8) for asset context.
visualization: Timeline (bootstrap attempts), Table (failed devices), Single value (open failures).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚ÄĘ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Cisco DC Networking Application (Splunkbase 7777), NDFC syslog.
‚ÄĘ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=cisco_dc` `sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:poap"` with fields `serial_number`, `switch_name`, `stage`, `status`, `error_code`.
‚ÄĘ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚ÄĒ Configure data collection
(1) Forward NDFC POAP/ZTP logs to Splunk with parsed `stage` milestones. (2) Alert on any failure before switch reaches `In-Sync`. (3) Join serial to inventory (UC-18.4.8) for asset context.

Step 2 ‚ÄĒ Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cisco_dc sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:poap" earliest=-7d
| where match(lower(status),"(?i)fail|error|timeout") OR (match(lower(stage),"(?i)image|cert|dhcp") AND status!="success")
| stats latest(_time) as last_fail latest(error_code) as err latest(stage) as stage by serial_number, switch_name
| sort -last_fail
| table last_fail, serial_number, switch_name, stage, err
```

Understanding this SPL

**NDFC POAP / ZTP Bootstrap and Day-0 Onboarding Failures** ‚ÄĒ Automated provisioning brings switches into the fabric quickly; DHCP, image fetch, or certificate failures during POAP/ZTP delay expansions and leave partially configured devices in racks. Monitoring onboarding outcomes keeps brownfield growth on schedule and prevents rogue devices from sitting outside policy.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=cisco_dc` `sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:poap"` with fields `serial_number`, `switch_name`, `stage`, `status`, `error_code`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Cisco DC Networking Application (Splunkbase 7777), NDFC syslog. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚ÄĒrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cisco_dc; **sourcetype**: cisco:ndfc:poap. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚ÄĘ Scopes the data: index=cisco_dc, sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:poap", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚ÄĘ Filters the current rows with `where match(lower(status),"(?i)fail|error|timeout") OR (match(lower(stage),"(?i)image|cert|dhcp") AND status!="success")` ‚ÄĒ typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚ÄĘ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by serial_number, switch_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚ÄĘ Orders rows with `sort` ‚ÄĒ combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
‚ÄĘ Pipeline stage (see **NDFC POAP / ZTP Bootstrap and Day-0 Onboarding Failures**): table last_fail, serial_number, switch_name, stage, err


Step 3 ‚ÄĒ Validate
Confirm results in the index, then compare with the same scope in the relevant fabric/SDN controller or manager UI.

Step 4 ‚ÄĒ Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (bootstrap attempts), Table (failed devices), Single value (open failures).
grandmaExplanation: Automated provisioning brings switches into the fabric quickly;, image fetch, or certificate failures during POAP/ZTP delay expansions and leave partially configured devices in racks ‚ÄĒ so traffic problems, routing mistakes, and risky changes surface fast.
knownFalsePositives: Faults during planned maintenance, hardware refresh, or after firmware upgrades.
